Title: Engineering Rubisco condensation in chloroplasts to manipulate plant photosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rubisco large subunit fused to superfolder GFP
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces liquid-like condensation of endogenous Rubisco in chloroplasts while maintaining CO2-fixation activity and supporting normal autotrophic growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: chloroplast transformation; Southern blot; SDS-PAGE/immunoblot; native-PAGE; confocal microscopy; TEM; FRAP; 14CO2-fixation assay; gas exchange
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fusion of sfGFP to NtRbcL generated homoplasmic transplastomic tobacco lines expressing NtRbcL-sfGFP. 2. NtRbcL-sfGFP assembled with native RbcS into L8S8 holoenzyme and formed larger condensate assemblies in chloroplasts. 3. Condensates were liquid-like and did not impair Rubisco catalytic activity or plant growth in ambient air.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn, constitutive plastid rRNA operon promoter
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ambient air; dark treatment; 1% CO2 exposure; greenhouse and MS medium growth
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No notable penalty in leaf number, plant height, dry weight, gas exchange, or life cycle under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a chloroplast-engineering scaffold to create synthetic pyrenoid/carboxysome-like condensates for improving photosynthetic efficiency
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an engineered fusion construct rather than a native gene; condensation is attributed to sfGFP self-oligomerization; terminal nutrient metabolite is not a classical nutritional compound
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native rbcL is chloroplast-expressed in leaves; engineered fusion expressed from plastome in leaves

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Selectable marker enabling spectinomycin-resistant plastid transformation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aadA was included in the chloroplast transformation vector. 2. It enabled recovery and selection of transplastomic tobacco lines. 3. It was not studied for metabolic causality.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as transformation marker only; not a nutrient-metabolism core gene
